verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p24delta1) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24 family primarily resident in the Golgi/ERGIC and cycling through the secretory pathway. The NCBI summary explicitly notes localization to both the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae, indicating a documented PM pool alongside a dominant endomembrane compartment — a dual_localization pattern. It traffics between Golgi and PM via vesicular cycling, so surface display is real but not the dominant steady-state localization. Contextual buckets checked: cell_state_induced — not the documented mechanism; tissue_restricted_surface — ubiquitously expressed; lysosomal_exocytosis — not documented; dual_localization — YES, fits: PM pool confirmed alongside dominant Golgi residence; stable_surface_attachment — not applicable (it is a TM protein, not secreted). Therefore dual_localization is the best fit for contextual verdict.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Quantitative fraction at PM vs. Golgi under baseline conditions is not precisely defined; some studies treat it primarily as Golgi-resident with minor PM cycling.
